body.title: Igor self-audit habit: scan rule-shaped memories for avoidance-frame, propose reframes
body.result: ApproachFrameAuditSource shipped: night-time periodic scan of rule-shaped memories, regex-scored, writes pending_approach_reframe FACTUAL candidates with TWM markers. 24 tests pass. Commit 75d2cb24.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Igor's memory corpus contains rule-shaped memories (habits, pe_chain cells, palace rule echoes) that likely carry proscriptive language. Need an ongoing process — not a one-shot sprint — where Igor audits these memories through the approach-frame lens, proposes reframes, and queues them for CC/Akien review.

**Pre-sprint discovery step (REQUIRED before coding):** identify the target file for the new habit/hook. Expected candidates: wild_igor/igor/tools/habit_engine.py, wild_igor/igor/brainstem/habit_runner.py, or similar. Update 'Affected files' in this ticket before writing any code. Docs-in-code: update that file's docstring to describe the new habit alongside the code change.

**Shape:** periodic habit (not one-shot — no deadline, housekeeping). Worker: igor. On each trigger: sample N rule-shaped memories from clan.memories (memory_type IN ('RULE','HABIT','PROCEDURE','SKILL')), score for approach-frame, flag avoidance-frame ones, write proposed reframe as a new pending memory in clan.memories (tagged pending_approach_reframe — no new table, reuse existing schema). CC/Akien drains the pending queue during normal sessions.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step first (see above). Expected: one file in wild_igor/igor/. Persistence: clan.memories (existing Postgres table, existing schema — new tag only).

**Design rules:** no-sqlite (Postgres only, clan.memories existing); no-new-memory-schemas (new tag on existing type, not new table); docs-in-code (update target file's docstring alongside code); test-plan-or-why-not — see below.

**Scope boundary:**
- In: habit/hook that samples rule-shaped memories, scores, proposes reframes, writes to pending queue.
- Out: actually applying reframes (CC review step). Out: non-rule memories.

**Gate:** T-approach-frame-sensor-node (metric must exist to report into).

**Test plan:** write test with 2 synthetic memories (one approach-framed, one proscriptive). Confirm: proscriptive → flagged + reframe written to pending queue. Approach-framed → passes without action. Use existing habit engine test patterns.
